executive_summary: Build workflow execution failed due to missing or inaccessible GitHub Actions workflow file. The workflow dispatch endpoint returned HTTP 404, indicating that the .github/workflows/build.yml file is either not present in the repository, not accessible with current permissions, or GitHub Actions is disabled for this repository.

errors.title: GitHub Actions Workflow File Not Found
errors.description: The workflow dispatch API endpoint returned HTTP 404 when attempting to trigger the build.yml workflow. This indicates a fundamental configuration issue preventing any build execution.
errors.error_message: HTTP Error 404: Not Found. Workflow dispatch endpoint returned 404. Possible causes: token missing workflow/actions permissions, workflow not accessible on this repo/branch, or Actions disabled. dispatch_url=https://api.github.com/repos/loginkarun/scib_demo/actions/workflows/.github%2Fworkflows%2Fbuild.yml/dispatches response_body={
errors.root_cause: The workflow file .github/workflows/build.yml does not exist in the repository at the specified branch (main), or the GitHub token lacks sufficient permissions to access/trigger workflows, or GitHub Actions is disabled for this repository.
errors.impact: Complete build pipeline failure. No builds can be executed until the workflow file is created or permissions are corrected. This blocks all CI/CD automation including compilation, testing, and deployment.

errors.title: Missing Workflow File Path
errors.description: The expected workflow file path .github/workflows/build.yml is not present in the repository structure.
errors.error_message: Workflow file not found - Final attempt failure
errors.root_cause: The .github/workflows directory may not exist, or the build.yml file was never created, or it was deleted/moved without updating references.
errors.impact: Prevents automated build triggers. Manual intervention required to create or restore the workflow file.

errors.title: GitHub Actions API Authentication/Permission Issue
errors.description: The GitHub token may lack the required 'workflow' or 'actions' permissions to dispatch workflow events.
errors.root_cause: The personal access token (PAT) or GitHub App token used for API calls may not have the 'workflow' scope enabled, which is required to trigger workflow_dispatch events.
errors.impact: Even if the workflow file exists, builds cannot be triggered programmatically. Requires token regeneration with proper scopes.

steps: 2. Create build.yml file with Maven build configuration
3. Include workflow_dispatch trigger to enable manual/API triggering
4. Commit and push to main branch
5. Verify workflow appears in Actions tab
1. Navigate to GitHub Settings > Developer settings > Personal access tokens
2. Generate new token or edit existing token
1. Navigate to repository Settings > Actions > General
2. Ensure 'Allow all actions and reusable workflows' is selected
3. Check that Actions are not disabled at organization level

compliance_notes.audit_trail: All workflow dispatch attempts logged with HTTP 404 responses. No successful builds executed.
compliance_notes.security_impact: No security vulnerabilities introduced. Configuration issue only.
compliance_notes.rollback_plan: N/A - No changes to rollback. Issue is missing configuration.

next_steps: 1. IMMEDIATE: Create .github/workflows/build.yml file with proper Maven build configuration
2. IMMEDIATE: Verify GitHub token has 'workflow' scope enabled
3. Verify GitHub Actions is enabled for the repository
4. Test workflow dispatch manually from Actions tab
5. Retry automated build trigger after fixes applied
